newsSince: Materially positive: ASE raised its 2026 capex a second time, from ~$7B to ~$8.5B (+20%+), lifted its LEAP advanced-packaging revenue target by >10% to over $3.5B, and said AI/CoWoS advanced-packaging revenue will beat initial expectations by >$250M on strong demand and TSMC packaging outsourcing. Fundamentals clearly strengthened, but the +16% move since June 9 and +156% YTD show much is already priced.

sources.label: BigGo Finance - ASE raises capex to $8.5B, lifts LEAP target
Digitimes - ASE advanced packaging/testing revenue to rise $1B in 2026

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha-vs-passives lens: the capex hike to ~$8.5B, +10% LEAP target raise and >$250M CoWoS beat genuinely strengthen the advanced-packaging bottleneck (+2 fundamentals). But +156% YTD, +16% spike and consensus Strong-Buy status mean the news is largely consumed; unpriced asymmetry shrank, so hold rank.
verdicts.bull: Capex raised twice; LEAP >$3.5B; CoWoS beating on TSMC outsourcing - structural packaging choke point.
verdicts.neutral: Strong fundamentals but now consensus and richly valued.
verdicts.bear: +156% YTD; valuation flags; H1 de-stocking; news already in the price.
